---
title: "General Encoder Model"
---

## Overview

`GeneralEncoderModel` is a reusable `ModelBase` subclass that wraps any image
encoder backbone (e.g. ConvNeXt, ViT, ResNet) as a single-input, single-output
model for spatial feature extraction. It maps an input image to a raw feature
tensor without requiring a task-specific C++ wrapper for each architecture.

Post-encoder processing (global pooling, spatial point extraction, etc.) is
**not** part of this model. Post-encoder modules are a separate, model-agnostic
pipeline stage owned by `SlotAssembler` and configurable for any loaded model.
See [Post-Encoder Modules](post_encoder_modules.qmd).

**Source:** `src/DeepLearning/models_v2/general_encoder/`

## Architecture

```
Input Image [B, C, H, W]  →  GeneralEncoderModel::forward()  →  Features [B, C_out, H_out, W_out]
         "image" slot              (any .pt/.pt2)                    "features" slot
                                                                    │
                                                                    ▼
                                              PostEncoderModule (optional, session config)
                                                                    │
                                                                    ▼
                                              Channel decoder (e.g. TensorToFeatureVector)
```

### Design decisions

`GeneralEncoderModel` is intentionally minimal:

- **Single input slot** (`"image"`) — accepts any image tensor
- **Single output slot** (`"features"`) — produces the raw backbone output
- **Configurable shapes** — input resolution and output feature shape are set at
  construction time or via registered model configuration
- **DynamicBatch** — `DynamicBatch{1, 0}` (min 1, unlimited max)

For most users, defining an encoder via a `RuntimeModelSpec` JSON file (which
creates a `RuntimeModel`) is sufficient and requires no C++ changes.
`GeneralEncoderModel` provides a convenience wrapper with sensible defaults that
auto-registers in `ModelRegistry` with a configuration schema.

### Compile-time vs. runtime shapes

Input and output resolution **do not need to be known at compile time:**

- **TorchScript** (`.pt`) accepts arbitrary dynamic shapes natively
- **AOT Inductor** (`.pt2`) supports dynamic shapes via `torch.export.Dim()` —
  dimensions marked as dynamic can vary at runtime within declared bounds
- **`RuntimeModel`** reads shapes from a JSON spec at runtime

The shapes declared in `GeneralEncoderModel` (or `RuntimeModelSpec`) are
**metadata for the UI and pre-allocation**, not hard constraints enforced at the
C++ level.

### Post-encoder recommendation

`GeneralEncoderModel::recommendedPostEncoderModule()` returns `"global_avg_pool"`.
The output slot recommends `TensorToFeatureVector` as its decoder — appropriate
after spatial collapse to `[B, C]`.

On model selection, `DeepLearningPropertiesWidget` pre-populates
`DeepLearningState::postEncoderParams()` from `ModelInfo::recommended_post_encoder`.
The user can override the module for any model in `PostEncoderWidget`.

## Usage

### Option 1: C++ `GeneralEncoderModel` (compiled)

The default constructor creates a model expecting 3×224×224 RGB input and
384×7×7 output:

```cpp
auto model = std::make_unique<dl::GeneralEncoderModel>();
model->loadWeights("/path/to/convnext_tiny_encoder.pt");

auto image = torch::randn({1, 3, 224, 224});
std::unordered_map<std::string, torch::Tensor> inputs;
inputs["image"] = image;

auto outputs = model->forward(inputs);
auto features = outputs["features"];  // [1, 384, 7, 7]
```

For a different backbone, pass custom dimensions:

```cpp
auto model = std::make_unique<dl::GeneralEncoderModel>(
    3, 224, 224, std::vector<int64_t>{768, 7, 7});
```

### Option 2: `RuntimeModelSpec` JSON (no recompilation)

Create a JSON spec file (see `examples/convnext_encoder_spec.json`):

```json
{
  "model_id": "convnext_tiny_encoder",
  "display_name": "ConvNeXt-Tiny Encoder",
  "weights_path": "convnext_tiny_encoder.pt",
  "batch_mode": { "dynamic": { "min": 1, "max": 32 } },
  "inputs": [
    { "name": "image", "shape": [3, 224, 224], "recommended_encoder": "ImageEncoder" }
  ],
  "outputs": [
    { "name": "features", "shape": [768, 7, 7], "recommended_decoder": "TensorToFeatureVector" }
  ],
  "recommended_post_encoder": "global_avg_pool"
}
```

Load via `ModelRegistry`:

```cpp
dl::ModelRegistry::instance().loadFromJson("/path/to/spec.json");
auto model = dl::ModelRegistry::instance().create("convnext_tiny_encoder");
```

### Option 3: Widget UI

`GeneralEncoderModel` appears in the Deep Learning widget model selector as
**General Encoder**. The standard workflow:

1. Select the model
2. Configure input/output shapes in the **Model Configuration** section
   (`EncoderShapeWidget`) and click **Apply Configuration**
3. Load weights
4. Configure the image binding and optional post-encoder module
5. Run inference

#### Runtime shape configuration

Different backbones produce different output shapes and may require different
input resolutions:

| Backbone | Typical input | Typical output |
|---|---|---|
| ConvNeXt-Tiny | 224×224 | 768×7×7 |
| ConvNeXt-Base | 224×224 | 1024×7×7 |
| EfficientViT | 256×256 | 192×16×16 |
| ViT-Base | 224×224 | 768×14×14 |

`EncoderShapeWidget` drives an `AutoParamWidget` form from
`GeneralEncoderModelParams`:

| Field | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `input_height` / `input_width` | Input resolution in pixels |
| `output_shape` | Comma-separated output dims (e.g. `"768,7,7"`) |
| `shape_applied` | Set when the user clicks Apply Configuration |

Configuration persists in
`DeepLearningStateData::model_configurations["general_encoder"]` and is applied
via `SlotAssembler::applyModelConfiguration()`.

Weights loading is blocked until `generalEncoderConfigurationComplete()` returns
true (`shape_applied` and positive height/width).

::: {.callout-important}
**AOT Inductor models compiled with fixed spatial dimensions will produce NaN
output if fed the wrong input size.** Match the input resolution to what was used
during `torch.export.export()`. When using `torch.export.Dim()` for dynamic
shapes, only the batch dimension is typically dynamic — spatial dimensions remain
fixed.
:::

#### Programmatic shape configuration

```cpp
auto model = std::make_unique<dl::GeneralEncoderModel>();
model->setInputResolution(256, 256);
model->setOutputShape({192, 16, 16});
model->loadWeights("/path/to/efficientvit_encoder.pt2");
```

Helpers in `GeneralEncoderConfiguration.hpp`:

- `applyGeneralEncoderConfiguration()` — apply params to a live instance
- `parseGeneralEncoderOutputShape()` — parse comma-separated shape string
- `generalEncoderConfigurationComplete()` — validate before weight load

## Exporting models

Use the provided Python export script:

```bash
python examples/export_convnext_encoder.py --model convnext_tiny --output-dir ./models
```

This produces:

- `convnext_tiny_encoder.pt` — TorchScript
- `convnext_tiny_encoder.pt2` — AOT Inductor (PyTorch 2.1+)
- `convnext_tiny_encoder_spec.json` — `RuntimeModelSpec`

See `examples/export_convnext_encoder.py --help` for all options.

## Slot descriptors

### Input: `"image"`

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Shape | `[C, H, W]` (default: `[3, 224, 224]`) |
| DType | `Float32` |
| Recommended encoder | `ImageEncoder` |
| Static | No |

### Output: `"features"`

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Shape | Configurable (default: `[384, 7, 7]`) |
| DType | `Float32` |
| Recommended decoder | `TensorToFeatureVector` (after post-encoder collapse) |
| Static | No |

`ModelBase::outputSlots()` always reports raw backbone shapes. Effective shapes
seen by decoders come from `effectiveOutputSlots()` after the session
post-encoder module is applied.

## Registration

`GeneralEncoderModel` self-registers via `DL_REGISTER_MODEL_WITH_CONFIG` at
static initialization. Registry ID: `"general_encoder"`.

The registration bundles:

- `GeneralEncoderModelParams` — configuration schema
- `applyGeneralEncoderConfiguration()` — apply params to instance
- `generalEncoderConfigurationComplete()` — weight-load gate
- Form/stored JSON converters for `AutoParamWidget`

## Files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `GeneralEncoderModel.hpp` / `.cpp` | Model wrapper |
| `GeneralEncoderModelParams.hpp` | Serializable configuration |
| `GeneralEncoderConfiguration.hpp` / `.cpp` | Apply/validate helpers |
| `GeneralEncoderParamSchemas.hpp` | `ParameterUIHints` for shape form |
| `tests/DeepLearning/models_v2/GeneralEncoderModel.test.cpp` | Unit tests |
| `examples/convnext_encoder_spec.json` | Example `RuntimeModelSpec` |
| `examples/export_convnext_encoder.py` | Python export script |

## Related documentation

- [Post-Encoder Modules](post_encoder_modules.qmd) — model-agnostic output processing
- [ModelInfo](models_v2/ModelInfo.qmd) — `recommended_post_encoder` metadata
- `EncoderShapeWidget` — `src/WhiskerToolbox/DeepLearning_Widget/UI/Helpers/EncoderShapeWidget.hpp`
